model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Adopt a stronger MLP configuration: wider/deeper layers, AdamW, larger batch size, and an enabled cosine schedule with step-aware timing should improve optimization under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries","topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Add one more hidden block to the tapering MLP and use label smoothing, which was present in a stronger prior CIFAR-10 MLP run. This keeps the optimization setup stable while giving the model a bit more capacity and a better generalization bias.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Revert the label smoothing that hurt validation loss in the last step. The rest of the configuration is already strong, so this is the lowest-risk correction before trying a different regularization change.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The layout is already strong; the remaining gap is likely optimization speed. A slightly higher learning rate and less conservative Adam second-moment averaging should help the model make better use of the fixed-step budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The LR bump did not help, so the next move is to restore the better optimizer settings and add one more hidden block. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"],"rationale":"The current depth-5, batch-512 variant did not beat the best result. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The current model is still fairly flat through the middle. A steeper taper with a smaller late bottleneck is a low-risk architectural regularization change and should better match stronger MLP shapes from the prior runs.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"],"rationale":"The tapering change did not help, so restore the stronger step-7 shape and trade compute for a larger batch. That is the most plausible way to tighten the validation loss from this point without adding instability.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The current large-batch setup is already the best performer so far. A further batch increase is the cleanest low-complexity adjustment to test whether this fixed-step run still benefits from lower gradient noise and more samples per update.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The batch-size increase improved the run substantially, so the next low-risk move is to let optimization move a bit faster under the same schedule. A small LR bump is the most direct way to test whether the current large-batch regime is still slightly under-driving the fit.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The large-batch run is still creeping down, which suggests the optimization step size may be slightly conservative. A small LR increase is the cleanest next sweep under the same stable architecture and schedule.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The LR bump slightly overshot the previous best, so restore the better step size and add only a mild amount of label smoothing. With the batch this large, a small regularization nudge is the safest next way to test for another drop.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The label smoothing clearly regressed the metric, so the safest next move is to revert it and keep the rest of the current large-batch setup unchanged.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The best results so far cluster tightly around the 1e-3 learning-rate regime. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The LR sweep is still improving as it moves slightly above 1e-3, and the current batch size is large enough to tolerate a modest further increase. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The 1.1e-3 step regressed slightly, and the strongest evidence so far is around 1.05e-3.
